hypotheses.title: Stiffness-Dependent Super-Enhancer Nucleation via YAP-Recruited BRD4-MED1 Phase Condensates
hypotheses.mechanism_route: ECM stiffness -> integrin/FAK -> LATS inhibition -> YAP nuclear -> TEAD/EP300 -> H3K27ac -> BRD4/MED1 condensate nucleation -> super-enhancer formation
hypotheses.novelty_claim: Stiff ECM triggers qualitative typical-to-super-enhancer phase transition at TEAD-dense loci via BRD4-MED1 condensate nucleation; 0 PubMed papers for ECM stiffness + super-enhancer
hypotheses.kill_vector: Phase separation at super-enhancers contested (McSwiggen 2019); BRD4 clusters may not be true condensates

hypotheses.title: Coordinated Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Conversion via Dual ECM-Stiffness-Controlled Enzymes (KDM6B + EP300)
hypotheses.mechanism_route: ECM stiffness -> KDM6B upregulation (removes H3K27me3) + YAP/EP300 activation (writes H3K27ac) -> coordinated bivalent-to-active enhancer switch at developmental loci
hypotheses.novelty_claim: ECM stiffness simultaneously controls both the eraser (KDM6B) and writer (EP300) acting on the same H3K27 residue at enhancers, enabling coordinated bivalent-to-active conversion; no paper combines both enzymes at enhancers under stiffness
hypotheses.kill_vector: KDM6B may not act at distal enhancers (only shown at promoters in Yu 2025); UTX/KDM6A may be the dominant enhancer demethylase

hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Reorganizes Enhancer-Promoter Loop Topology via LINC-Mediated Cohesin Dynamics
hypotheses.mechanism_route: ECM stiffness -> actin tension -> LINC complex (SUN2/LMNA) -> nuclear chromatin tension -> altered cohesin loop extrusion kinetics -> enhancer-promoter loop reorganization at YAP target TADs
hypotheses.novelty_claim: ECM stiffness reorganizes enhancer-promoter loops via force-altered cohesin dynamics; 0 PubMed papers for Hi-C under ECM stiffness gradients
hypotheses.kill_vector: TADs are remarkably stable across perturbations (Rao 2017); cohesin dynamics under tension unmeasured; force may dissipate before reaching interior loci

hypotheses.title: LAD-Embedded Enhancers as Stiffness-Resistant Mechanical Selectivity Filter
hypotheses.mechanism_route: ECM stiffness -> lamin A/C upregulation -> reinforced LAD silencing at periphery + YAP/EP300 activation at interior -> binary partition of enhancers into stiffness-responsive (non-LAD) and stiffness-resistant (LAD)
hypotheses.novelty_claim: LAD organization creates a binary selectivity filter determining which enhancers can respond to ECM stiffness; extends Sun 2020 gene-level finding to enhancer-resolution genomic architecture
hypotheses.kill_vector: LINC forces act at nuclear periphery where LADs are; if lamin A/C upregulation causes LAD release rather than strengthening, prediction inverts

hypotheses.title: Enhancer-Encoded Mechanical Memory via Self-Reinforcing H3K27ac-BRD4-EP300 Positive Feedback
hypotheses.mechanism_route: ECM stiffness -> YAP -> EP300 -> H3K27ac -> BRD4 recruitment -> BRD4 stabilizes EP300 -> self-reinforcing loop maintains H3K27ac after YAP relocalization -> mechanical memory at enhancers
hypotheses.novelty_claim: Specific molecular mechanism for mechanical memory at enhancer resolution: EP300-BRD4 positive feedback loop maintains stiffness-programmed H3K27ac after transfer to soft ECM; Hsia 2023 proposed concept but no experimental test at enhancer resolution exists
hypotheses.kill_vector: H3K27ac rapid turnover (~2-6 hr half-life) may overwhelm feedback loop; YAP/nuclear actin may be the actual memory element (Yang et al., Nat Mater 2014)
